Yeah. I feel like I’m “extreme” in my head but what I show out towards the mainstream world aka social media is the complete opposite of extreme 👀 Closeted, yes. But more for the fear of no response than for the fear of backlash..

![Christina DiStefano](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/8f211129a96f46953cf5ac3f42a75119?d=mm&s=70)

Christina DiStefano

Awaiting Review 

2 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/11362971/comments/24325480)

I create YouTube videos about interior design. Since I started last December I have gone in two different directions. At first I just made some videos because I was tired of waiting for the right time. Then I made a video that was a conversation about what home should be to me. That took off and from there I started making the same basic type of videos that everyone else makes—the steps videos—the tips videos. This helped me build my audience but to my credit I did make these ideas unique to myself—yet I still think they’re what’s expected from this platform as a “How to” video. It’s now July and I am tired of making this type of content. I mean how many times does someone need a video on designing a small living room? I can’t keep regurgitating the same ideas like many of the other very successful YouTubers in my genre do. It’s just another meaningless job to me in that way. Perhaps it’s because I am an artist that I must find meaning in what I do and it must be expressive of my ever evolving self? I have done all that the YT experts say I should do to grow my channel and it worked. Except now I want to flip the table over sometimes because it feels like a trap. You mentioned creating for the algorithm. Yep exactly. In all fairness I have formed a community that is maybe 1/9th the number of subscribers that actually watch everything I make. They are the true fans and they like it when I get creative and expressive. They want new ideas. Those are the people I want to make videos for—which incidentally, they are the artists that are searching for help in designing their home. The design world has told them it should be THIS WAY and I’m telling them, it should be YOUR WAY. Be creative. Trust yourself. Don’t fall into generic advice. I know this is the right path. I’m here to open my creative channel (access the source of my creativity within, let her out, let her speak) but I would be lying if I didn’t say that I worry about being misunderstood and dismissed. Right now I’m experimenting with what an interior design video should look like. I don’t want it to look like everything else I see other design YouTubers doing but it must speak to people looking for that guidance. So I’m playing and having fun for the first time in months! I can feel there’s so much more that could come out but there’s a block. It’s only leaking out. I’m here to open up those flood gates, baby.

* * *

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/11362971/comments/24023403)

I guess it's time to peacock my message more than ever. To have a goal that feels way bigger than me and my art. To finally get my studio. Reach for independence through my art. So that one day it doesn't become just something aesthetically pleasing. So that it becomes "The Aesthetic". The Message. The Community. The Symbol of Creative Freedom. Time to get to work. IAMSVRREAL [www.iamsvrreal.com](http://www.iamsvrreal.com) [www.instagram.com/iamsur_real](http://www.instagram.com/iamsur_real)

'I prefer to be on the wrong side of common sense if I'm on the right side of making history' perfectly describes a feeling I've had inside me but have never been able to put into words. Thank you for this message, I'll let it marinate and see how I can incorporate it into my art!
